title: श्री दशावतार ध्यानम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री दशावतार ध्यानम् ॥

ॐ श्री विष्णवे नमः।

॥ मत्स्यावतार ध्यानम् ॥
वन्दे मत्स्यावतारं तं सुवर्णवर्णशोभितम्।
चतुर्भुजं शङ्खचक्रगदापद्मधरं हरिम्॥
प्रलयार्णवमग्नानां वेदानामुद्धरं विभुम्।
ध्यायेत्सर्वजगन्नाथं मत्स्यरूपं सनातनम्॥ १॥

॥ कूर्मावतार ध्यानम् ॥
ध्यायेत्कूर्मस्वरूपं तं मन्दराद्रिधरं हरिम्।
क्षीरसागरमध्यस्थं सर्वदेवनमस्कृतम्॥
पृष्ठेन धारयन्तं तं गिरिं मन्थनकारणम्।
कूर्मरूपधरं विष्णुं प्रणमामि मुदान्वितः॥ २॥

॥ वराहावतार ध्यानम् ॥
नीलमेघनिभं देवं वराहं भूमिधारिणम्।
दंष्ट्राग्रे धृतभूदेवीं हिरण्याक्षनिषूदनम्॥
प्रणमामि वराहं तं भक्तानामभयप्रदम्।
यज्ञवाराहमीशानं सर्वलोकहिते रतम्॥ ३॥

॥ नृसिंहावतार ध्यानम् ॥
सटाजूटसमायुक्तं रक्तनेत्रं महाबलम्।
ज्वालामालासमाकीर्णं नृसिंहं भयनाशनम्॥
स्तम्भात्प्रादुर्भवं देवं हिरण्यकशिपोर्वधम्।
प्रह्लादवरदं वन्दे नृहरिं करुणानिधिम्॥ ४॥

॥ वामनावतार ध्यानम् ॥
वामनं छत्रदण्डं च कमण्डलुधरं शुभम्।
ब्रह्मचारिस्वरूपेण बलियज्ञगतं हरिम्॥
त्रिविक्रमं महाविष्णुं त्रैलोक्यव्यापिनं विभुम्।
वामनं प्रणमाम्यादिं दैत्यदर्पविनाशनम्॥ ५॥

॥ परशुरामावतार ध्यानम् ॥
जटामण्डलसंयुक्तं तपस्वीरूपमव्ययम्।
परशुं दक्षिणे हस्ते धनुर्बाणधरं शुभम्॥
क्षत्रियान्तकरं वीरं जमदग्निसुतं हरिम्।
भार्गवं प्रणमाम्यादिं परशुरामं नमाम्यहम्॥ ६॥

॥ श्रीरामावतार ध्यानम् ॥
नीलोत्पलदलश्यामं पद्मपत्रायतेक्षणम्।
सीतालक्ष्मणसंयुक्तं हनूमत्सेवितं सदा॥
कोदण्डधारिणं रामं रावणान्तकमीश्वरम्।
ध्यायेद्दाशरथिं देवं मर्यादापुरुषोत्तमम्॥ ७॥

॥ बलरामावतार ध्यानम् ॥
गौरवर्णं महाबाहुं हलमुसलधारिणम्।
नीलवस्त्रधरं देवं रेवतीप्रियवल्लभम्॥
सहस्रफणसंयुक्तं शेषावतारमच्युतम्।
बलभद्रं प्रणम्यादिं हलायुधमनामयम्॥ ८॥

॥ श्रीकृष्णावतार ध्यानम् ॥
मेघश्यामं चतुर्बाहुं शङ्खचक्रगदाधरम्।
पीताम्बरधरं देवं वनमालाविभूषितम्॥
गोपगोपीजनप्रीतं मुरलीमधुरस्वनम्।
कृष्णं वन्दे जगन्नाथं देवकीनन्दनं हरिम्॥ ९॥

॥ कल्क्यवतार ध्यानम् ॥
श्वेताश्वसमारूढं खड्गहस्तं महाप्रभम्।
म्लेच्छनाशकरं वीरं कल्किरूपं सनातनम्॥
धर्मसंस्थापनार्थाय सम्भविष्यन्तमच्युतम्।
कल्किं प्रणमाम्यादिं विष्णुमन्त्यावतारकम्॥ १०॥

॥ सम्पूर्ण विनियोग ॥
इत्येवं दशधा रूपं धृत्वा विष्णुर्जनार्दनः।
धर्मं संस्थापयामास जगतां हितकाम्यया॥
दशावतारध्यानं यः पठेद्भक्तिसंयुतः।
सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तो विष्णुलोकं स गच्छति॥

॥ इति श्री दशावतार ध्यानम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Dashavatara Dhyanam ||

Om Shri Vishnave Namah.

|| Matsyavatara Dhyanam ||
Vande Matsya-avataram tam suvarna-varna-shobhitam.
Chaturbhujam Shankha-Chakra-Gada-Padma-dharam Harim.
Pralaya-arnava-magnanam Vedanam-uddharam Vibhum.
Dhyayet-sarva-Jagannatham Matsya-rupam Sanatanam.|| 1 ||

|| Kurmavatara Dhyanam ||
Dhyayet-Kurma-svarupam tam Mandara-adri-dharam Harim.
Kshira-sagara-madhya-stham sarva-deva-namaskritam.
Prishthena dharayantam tam girim manthana-karanam.
Kurma-rupa-dharam Vishnum pranamami mudanvitah.|| 2 ||

|| Varahavatara Dhyanam ||
Nila-megha-nibham Devam Varaham Bhumi-dharinam.
Damshtra-agre dhrita-Bhu-Devim Hiranyaksha-nishudanam.
Pranamami Varaham tam bhaktanam-abhaya-pradam.
Yajna-Varaham-Ishanam sarva-loka-hite ratam.|| 3 ||

|| Nrisimhavatara Dhyanam ||
Sata-juta-samayuktam rakta-netram Maha-balam.
Jvala-mala-samakeernam Nrisimham bhaya-nashanam.
Stambhat-pradurbhavam Devam Hiranyakashipor-vadham.
Prahlada-varadam vande Nriharim Karuna-nidhim.|| 4 ||

|| Vamanavatara Dhyanam ||
Vamanam Chhatra-dandam cha Kamandalu-dharam shubham.
Brahmachari-svarupena Bali-yajna-gatam Harim.
Trivikramam Maha-Vishnum Trailokya-vyapinam Vibhum.
Vamanam pranamamy-adim Daitya-darpa-vinashanam.|| 5 ||

|| Parashurama Avatara Dhyanam ||
Jata-mandala-samyuktam Tapasvi-rupam-avyayam.
Parashum dakshine haste Dhanur-bana-dharam shubham.
Kshatriya-antakaram Viram Jamadagni-sutam Harim.
Bhargavam pranamamy-adim Parashuramam namamyaham.|| 6 ||

|| Shri Ramavatara Dhyanam ||
Nilotpala-dala-shyamam Padma-patra-ayata-ikshanam.
Sita-Lakshmana-samyuktam Hanumat-sevitam sada.
Kodanda-dharinam Ramam Ravana-antakam-Ishvaram.
Dhyayed-Dasharathim Devam Maryada-Purushottamam.|| 7 ||

|| Balarama Avatara Dhyanam ||
Gaura-varnam Maha-bahum Hala-Musala-dharinam.
Nila-vastra-dharam Devam Revati-priya-vallabham.
Sahasra-phana-samyuktam Shesha-avataram-Achyutam.
Balabhadram pranamyadim Halayudham-anamayam.|| 8 ||

|| Shri Krishnavatara Dhyanam ||
Megha-shyamam Chatur-bahum Shankha-Chakra-Gada-dharam.
Pitambara-dharam Devam Vanamala-vibhushitam.
Gopa-Gopi-jana-preetam Murali-madhura-svanam.
Krishnam vande Jagannatham Devaki-nandanam Harim.|| 9 ||

|| Kalki Avatara Dhyanam ||
Shveta-ashva-samarudham Khadga-hastam Maha-prabham.
Mlechchha-nashakaram Viram Kalki-rupam Sanatanam.
Dharma-samsthapanar-thaya sambhavishyantam-Achyutam.
Kalkim pranamamy-adim Vishnum-antya-avataarakam.|| 10 ||

|| Sampurna Viniyogah ||
Ity-evam dashadha rupam dhritva Vishnur-Janardanah.
Dharmam samsthapayas-masa jagatam hita-kamyaya.
Dashavatara-dhyanam yah pathed-bhakti-samyutah.
Sarva-papa-vinirmukto Vishnu-lokam sa gachchhati.

|| Iti Shri Dashavatara Dhyanam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: इस ध्यान में भगवान विष्णु के दस अवतारों का ध्यान किया जाता है। मत्स्य — सुवर्णवर्ण, चतुर्भुज, प्रलय-सागर में डूबे वेदों के उद्धारक। कूर्म — मन्दराचल को पीठ पर धारण करने वाले। वराह — नीलमेघ सदृश, दाढ़ पर पृथ्वी को धारण करने वाले, हिरण्याक्ष के संहारक। नृसिंह — ज्वालामाला से आवृत, भय-नाशक, प्रह्लाद को वर देने वाले। वामन — ब्रह्मचारी रूप में बलि के यज्ञ में गए त्रिविक्रम। परशुराम — परशुधारी, जमदग्निपुत्र, क्षत्रियान्तक। श्रीराम — कोदण्डधारी, सीता-लक्ष्मण-हनुमान सहित, मर्यादा पुरुषोत्तम। बलराम — गौरवर्ण, हल-मूसलधारी, शेषावतार। श्रीकृष्ण — मेघश्याम, मुरलीधर, गोप-गोपी-प्रिय, देवकीनन्दन। कल्कि — श्वेताश्व पर आरूढ़, खड्गधारी, म्लेच्छनाशक, धर्मसंस्थापक। जो इस ध्यान को भक्तिपूर्वक पढ़ता है वह सम्पूर्ण पापों से मुक्त होकर विष्णुलोक को जाता है।
benefits: Invokes the protective energy of all ten incarnations of Lord Vishnu
Invokes meditation-worthy vision of each avatara's divine form
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins and grants liberation to vishnu-loka
Invokes the blessing of dharmic stability and cosmic protection
Especially potent on Ekadashi, Janmashtami and Rama Navami
Develops deep devotion through contemplation of divine descents
description: Dashavatara Dhyanam — श्री दशावतार ध्यानम् — is a sacred prayer offered to Lord Vishnu. Attributed to Bhagavata Purana / Traditional, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to grant meditation-worthy vision of each avatara's divine form and invoke the protective energy of all ten incarnations of Lord Vishnu. It is also said to destroy all sins and grant liberation to Vishnu-loka. For best results, devotees chant this on Thursday (Guruvar) and Wednesday (Budhvar) and during Ekadashi, Janmashtami, and Ram Navami. Comprising 10 verses, it can be completed in approximately 12 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Dashavatara Dhyanam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Dashavatara Dhyanam in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. Meditative verses on all ten incarnations of Lord Vishnu.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Dashavatara Dhyanam?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dashavatara Dhyanam (श्री दशावतार ध्यानम्) is a comprehensive ten-verse Sanskrit meditation prayer offering individual dhyana-shlokas to each of the ten avataras of Lord Vishnu. Unlike the famous Jayadeva Dashavatara Stotra (Pralaya-payodhi-jale) of the Gita Govinda which is a hymn-of-praise, this Dhyanam is specifically a meditation guide — providing the visual iconographic form (dhyana-rupa) of each avatara so the devotee can mentally visualize and meditate upon each in succession. The ten meditations cover: (1) Matsya the golden fish saving the Vedas; (2) Kurma the tortoise supporting Mount Mandara; (3) Varaha the boar lifting the earth on his tusk; (4) Narasimha the man-lion bursting from the pillar; (5) Vamana the dwarf-Brahmana growing into Trivikrama; (6) Parashurama the axe-wielding warrior-sage; (7) Sri Rama the Maryada Purushottama with bow; (8) Balarama with plough and pestle; (9) Sri Krishna the flute-playing cowherd; (10) Kalki on the white horse. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 1.3; classical Vaishnava dhyana tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the ten avataras of Vishnu in the standard Dashavatara list?
seoFaqs.answer: The standard Dashavatara list, established in the Bhagavata Purana (1.3) and the Garuda Purana, comprises in chronological order: (1) Matsya — fish, who saved Manu and the Vedas during the great deluge; (2) Kurma — tortoise, who supported Mount Mandara during the Samudra Manthan; (3) Varaha — boar, who lifted the earth (Bhumi-devi) from the cosmic ocean and slew Hiranyaksha; (4) Narasimha — man-lion, who emerged from a pillar to slay Hiranyakashipu and protect Prahlada; (5) Vamana — dwarf-Brahmana, who covered the three worlds in three steps to humble King Bali; (6) Parashurama — axe-wielding warrior, son of Jamadagni, who exterminated the corrupt Kshatriyas twenty-one times; (7) Sri Rama — the perfect king of Ayodhya, slayer of Ravana; (8) Balarama (or Buddha in some traditions) — Krishna's elder brother with plough; (9) Sri Krishna — the supreme avatara of Dvapara yuga; (10) Kalki — the future avatara on a white horse who will end the Kali yuga. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 1.3; Garuda Purana 1.86; Matsya Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: How is Dashavatara Dhyanam different from Jayadeva's Dashavatara Stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: Both are tenfold Sanskrit compositions on Vishnu's avataras but differ profoundly in style, source and ritual function. Jayadeva's Dashavatara Stotra (the famous Pralaya-payodhi-jale dhritavanasi vedam) is from the Gita Govinda (12th century, court of King Lakshmana Sena of Bengal) — a poetic-musical composition originally meant for sung kirtana, with each verse ending in the refrain Keshava-dhrita-X-sharira jaya jagadisha hare; it is highly devotional and lyrical. The Dashavatara Dhyanam is a meditation manual — each of its ten verses provides the iconographic dhyana-rupa (form to visualise) of one avatara so the devotee can mentally enthrone and worship each form successively, suitable for archana and dhyana. Jayadeva's stotra is for kirtan, this Dhyanam is for dhyana. Together they form the complete classical Dashavatara liturgy. (Sources: Gita Govinda by Jayadeva; Vaishnava dhyana paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Dashavatara Dhyanam be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dashavatara Dhyanam is most powerful on Thursday (Guruvar — sacred to Vishnu) and Wednesday (Budhvar). The most auspicious days of the year are: every Ekadashi (especially Vaikuntha Ekadashi in Margashirsha, Devuthani Ekadashi in Kartik, Ashadhi-Kartiki Ekadashis), Krishna Janmashtami (Bhadrapada Krishna Ashtami), Ram Navami (Chaitra Shukla Navami), Narasimha Jayanti (Vaishakha Shukla Chaturdashi), Vamana Jayanti (Bhadrapada Shukla Dwadashi), Parashurama Jayanti (Vaishakha Shukla Tritiya = Akshaya Tritiya), Matsya Jayanti (Chaitra Shukla Tritiya), Kurma Jayanti (Vaishakha Purnima), Varaha Jayanti (Bhadrapada Shukla Tritiya), Balarama Jayanti (Shravana Krishna Shashthi). It is also recited as a daily morning prayer for comprehensive Vishnu-protection. (Sources: Drikpanchang Vishnu avatara jayanti calendar.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the order of avataras and why is each yuga associated with specific incarnations?
seoFaqs.answer: The ten avataras span the four yugas: Satya yuga — Matsya, Kurma, Varaha, Narasimha (the four pre-human avataras emerging during cosmic crises); Treta yuga — Vamana, Parashurama, Sri Rama (the human and dharma-restoring avataras); Dvapara yuga — Balarama (or Buddha in some traditions), Sri Krishna (the cowherd-prince avataras); Kali yuga — Kalki (the future avatara still to come at the close of Kali). The progression also represents an evolutionary arc: aquatic (Matsya), amphibian (Kurma), mammalian-amphibious (Varaha), part-human (Narasimha), dwarf-human (Vamana), early-human-warrior (Parashurama), perfect-civilised-king (Rama), agricultural (Balarama), pastoral-divine (Krishna), and apocalyptic-future (Kalki) — long noted by modern commentators as a parallel to biological and civilisational evolution. Each avatara descends to restore dharma in its specific cosmic context. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 1.3; Bhagavad Gita 4.7-8 yada yada hi dharmasya glanir-bhavati.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does daily recitation of the Dashavatara Dhyanam grant?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti of the Dashavatara Dhyanam declares: Dashavatara-dhyanam yah pathed-bhakti-samyutah, sarva-papa-vinirmukto Vishnu-lokam sa gachchhati — the one who recites this Dashavatara Dhyanam with devotion is freed from all sins and attains Vishnu-loka. Tradition further attributes the following: (1) protection from all ten cosmic dangers — each avatara grants its specific protection (Matsya from drowning/floods, Kurma from instability, Varaha from earth-related calamities, Narasimha from enemies and evil forces, Vamana from arrogance, Parashurama from injustice, Rama from adharma, Balarama from agricultural failure, Krishna from grief, Kalki from end-times tribulation); (2) sarva-papa-vinasha — destruction of all sins; (3) Vishnu-loka prapti — attainment of Vaikuntha; (4) dharmic stability and cosmic protection; (5) deep bhakti through systematic contemplation of Vishnu's complete divine descent. (Sources: Dashavatara Dhyanam phalashruti; Bhagavata Purana.)
